}}

The '''Bermuda national football team''' represents [[Bermuda]] ([[British Overseas Territories|British overseas territory]]) in men's international [[Association football|football]], which is governed by the [[Bermuda Football Association]] founded in 1928. It has been a member of [[FIFA]] and [[CONCACAF]] since 1962. Regionally, it is a member of [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] in the Caribbean Zone.

Bermuda has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]], but has qualified once for the [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]] in [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]], and has also participated once in League A and three times in League B of the [[CONCACAF Nations League]].

Bermuda's debut in international competitions was in the [[Football at the 1967 Pan American Games|1967 Pan American Games]]. Their first appearance in World Cup qualifiers was in the [[1970 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1970 CONCACAF qualification]]. The team achieved its first victory in 1967, defeating [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] 3–1.

==History==
===1964–1992===
Bermuda played their first international match on 10 August 1964, against [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]] in [[Reykjavík]], ending in a narrow victory to the Icelanders, 4–3. A few years later, Bermuda participated in the [[Football at the 1967 Pan American Games|1967 Pan American Games]] in [[Winnipeg]]. Coached by [[Graham Adams]], they reached the final but fell to [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]], 4–0 [[Overtime (sports)|a.e.t]].<ref name="Winnipeg">{{cite web|author=Erik Francisco Lugo, Eduardo Mendoza and Steve Torres|date=2 September 2021|title=V PANAMERICAN GAMES 1967 (Winnipeg) - Match Details|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesp/panam67det.html|website=[[RSSSF]]|location=|publisher=|access-date=17 October 2025}}</ref>

In 1968 they participated in the qualifiers for the [[1970 FIFA World Cup]] for the first time. In Group 1 of the first round, they were paired with [[United States men's national soccer team|United States]] and [[Canada men's national soccer team|Canada]]. They finished the group with one draw (against Canada, 0–0) and three losses and were eliminated. The sixties would end with an elimination at the hands of Mexico, in the [[1969 CONCACAF Championship qualification]] preliminary round, although Bermuda, after having lost 3–0 in [[Mexico City]], managed to defeat the Aztecs 2–1 in [[Hamilton, Bermuda|Hamilton]], on 2 November 1969, with goals from Albert Dowling and Bernard Brangman.<ref>{{cite web |author=Eduardo López |date=11 October 2019 |title=Bernard Brangman no recuerda el partido que lo hizo leyenda |url=https://mexico.as.com/mexico/2019/10/12/futbol/1570836306_161651.html |trans-title=Bernard Brangman doesn't remember the game that made him a legend |website=[[Diario AS]] |language=es |location= |publisher= |access-date=16 October 2025}}</ref>

The '70s would begin with another elimination in the first round of the [[1971 CONCACAF Championship qualification]], again at the hands of Mexico, which prevailed comfortably with an overall result of 6–0. In this decade the Bermuda team managed to win two consecutive bronze medals in the [[Central American and Caribbean Games]] of [[Football at the 1974 Central American and Caribbean Games|1974]] and [[Football at the 1978 Central American and Caribbean Games|1978]].<ref name="CAG74">{{cite web|author=Erik Francisco Lugo and Eduardo Mendoza|date=14 March 2023|title=Central American and Caribbean Games 1974 (Dominican Republic) - Match Details|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cag74det.html|website=RSSSF|location=|publisher=|access-date=18 October 2025}}</ref><ref name="CAG78">{{cite web|author=Erik Francisco Lugo and Eduardo Mendoza|date=14 March 2023|title=Central American and Caribbean Games 1978 (Colombia) - Match Details|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cag78det.html|website=RSSSF|location=|publisher=|access-date=18 October 2025}}</ref> The decade of the '80s was not very significant for the ''Gombey Warriors'' since they only participated in the [[Football at the 1982 Central American and Caribbean Games|1982 Central American and Caribbean Games]], a tournament where reached 4th place. This would be their final participation in the Games.

On the other hand, between 1967 and 1987, the Bermuda team played several qualifying matches for the [[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic soccer tournament]] but never qualified for the final tournament. In this period they experienced some of their worst defeats, such as two 8–0 results against Canada and Mexico.

===1992–2019===
Absent from the World Cup qualifiers between 1972 and 1992, Bermuda returned to the [[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1994 preliminary tournament]] where they eliminated [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]] in the first round thanks to the [[away goals rule]], after winning 1–0 in Hamilton (goal by [[Shaun Goater]]) and lost 2–1 in [[Port-au-Prince]]. Bermuda advanced to the second phase, in group B with [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]], [[Canada men's national soccer team|Canada]] and [[El Salvador national football team|El Salvador]], finishing in last place with 4 points, despite managing a historic 1–0 victory against the ''Selecta'', the result of a goal from [[Kyle Lightbourne]].<ref>{{cite web|author=<!-- not stated -->|date=19 October 1992|title=Lightbourne rocks El Salvador|url=https://www.royalgazette.com/other/sport/article/20110208/lightbourne-rocks-el-salvador/|website=The Royal Gazette|location=|publisher=|access-date=16 October 2025}}</ref> In the [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1998 World Cup qualifiers]], Bermuda withdrew without playing [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] in the first round.

The ''Gombey Warriors'' would return four years later for the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2002 World Cup qualifiers]] where they eliminated the [[British Virgin Islands national football team|British Virgin Islands]] in the first round, 5–1 and 9–0, before falling in the next phase, at the hands of [[Antigua and Barbuda national football team|Antigua and Barbuda]], who won thanks to the away goal rule after drawing 0–0 at [[St. John's, Antigua and Barbuda|Saint John's]] and 1–1 in Hamilton. In the tournament preliminary route to the [[2006 FIFA World Cup]], Bermuda got their biggest win, thrashing [[Montserrat national football team|Montserrat]] 13–0.<ref>{{cite web|author=Adrian Robson|date=21 March 2004|title=It's more than just about goals?|url=https://www.royalgazette.com/other/sport/article/20110204/its-more-than-just-about-goals/|website=The Royal Gazette|location=|publisher=|access-date=15 October 2025}}</ref> Later it was eliminated by El Salvador who prevailed 2–1 in San Salvador and later drew 2–2 in Hamilton. In the qualifying process for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]], Bermuda eliminated the [[Cayman Islands national football team|Cayman Islands]] in the first round with an aggregate score of 4–1. But it was brought down in the next round by Trinidad and Tobago with an aggregate score of 3–2 although Bermuda achieved an away victory, in [[Macoya]], against the ''Soca Warriors'' (1–2), with a double from [[John Nusum]]. In the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2014 World Cup qualifiers]], Bermuda entered the second round directly in group B, alongside [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]], Trinidad and Tobago and [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]]. Although they again defeated Trinidad and Tobago (2–1), they could only finish third in the group with 10 points, failing to advance to the third round.

On the other hand, since 1997, Bermuda began to play regularly in the knockout phase of the [[Caribbean Cup]] (except in 2001 and 2010) although it never managed to qualify for the final round. Their best performance occurred in the [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007 tournament]] where they advanced to the second knockout round. They missed the chance to reach the final tournament, losing to Haiti in two sets (0–2 and 0–3).<ref>{{cite web|author=<!-- not stated -->|date=9 January 2007|title=Bermuda defeated in Carib Cup|url=https://www.royalgazette.com/other/sport/article/20110204/bermuda-defeated-in-carib-cup/|website=The Royal Gazette|location=|publisher=|access-date=17 October 2025}}</ref>

In July 2013, Bermuda won its 15th gold medal at the [[2013 Island Games]] in Hamilton after beating [[Greenland national football team|Greenland]] 1–0.<ref>{{cite web|author=Hans Schöggl|date=23 September 2015|title=Island Games Tournament 2013 (Bermuda)|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesi/islandgames2013.html|website=RSSSF|location=|publisher=|access-date=17 October 2025}}</ref> This competition is not officially recognised because teams not recognized by FIFA participate.

[[File:Luke Robinson 2023.pdf|thumb|200px|right|[[Luke Robinson (Bermudan footballer)|Luke Robinson]] made his international debut for Bermuda in 2021 ]]

In 2015 Bermuda participated in the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2018 World Cup Qualifiers]] where they would face the [[Bahamas national football team|Bahamas]], whom they defeated over the two matches by 5–0. Going into the second round, they faced [[Guatemala national football team|Guatemala]], drawing 0–0 then falling 1–0, failing to reach the third round. Bermuda would return to the [[2017 Caribbean Cup qualification|Caribbean Cup]] after being absent in 2014, losing initially against [[Cuba national football team|Cuba]] 2–1 but defeating [[French Guiana national football team|French Guiana]] 2–1 to edge through to the second round on goal-difference. They then lost to both [[Dominican Republic national football team|Dominican Republic]] (2–1) and French Guiana (3–0) and were eliminated from the cup.

=== 2019–present ===
==== Debut in CONCACAF Gold Cup ====
In 2019, Bermuda made its [[CONCACAF Nations League]] debut, where it made history by finishing in the top ten of the [[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League qualifying|competition]], allowing it to jointly qualify for League A of the competition and its first [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Andrew Georgeson|date=25 March 2019|title=Reggie Lambe's Bermuda qualify for first ever Gold Cup|url=https://www.cambridge-news.co.uk/sport/reggie-lambes-bermuda-qualify-first-16023073|website=Cambridgeshire Live|location=|publisher=|access-date=16 October 2025}}</ref> In [[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League#League A|League A]], Bermuda were drawn with [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]] and [[Panama national football team|Panama]] but finished at the bottom of the group on goal-difference. 

At the [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup]], Bermuda is drawn in Group B with [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]], [[Costa Rica national football team|Costa Rica]] and [[Nicaragua national football team|Nicaragua]]. After two defeats to the first two teams, Bermuda won their first victory of the continental tournament by beating Nicaragua 2–0.<ref>{{cite web|author=<!-- not stated -->|date=24 June 2019|title=Bermuda downs Nicaragua to earn first ever Gold Cup victory|url=https://www.concacaf.com/en/gold-cup/news/bermuda-downs-nicaragua-to-earn-first-ever-gold-cup-victory/|website=Concacaf|location=|publisher=|access-date=15 October 2025}}</ref>

Even if Bermuda fails to qualify for the [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup qualification|2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup]], [[Nahki Wells]] made history by scoring the fastest Gold Cup goal (all phases combined) after 17 seconds in the match against [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] on 2 July 2021 (an 8–1 victory in the end).<ref>{{cite web|author=<!-- not stated -->|date=2 July 2021|title=Video: Wells Makes History With 17 Second Goal|url=https://bernews.com/2021/07/video-wells-makes-history-17-second-goal/|website=Bernews|location=|publisher=|access-date=16 October 2025}}</ref>

==== FIFA Series tournament ====
In 2024, FIFA Invited Bermuda to the [[2024 FIFA Series]] matches from 22 to 26 March 2024 held in [[Jeddah]]. On 22 March 2024, Bermuda faced off against [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]] from the [[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]], where they defeated them 2–0.

==== 2026 FIFA World Cup qualifiers ====

In 2024-2025, Bermuda participated in the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2026 World Cup Qualifiers]], entering the qualifiers at the second round. In their first two games in June 2024, they drew 1–1 with [[Antigua and Barbuda national football team|Antigua and Barbuda]] but lost 6–1 to [[Honduras national football team|Honduras]]. Then in their second two games in June 2025, they would defeat the [[Cayman Islands national football team|Cayman Islands]] 5–0 and then defeat [[Cuba national football team|Cuba]] 2–1 to finish second in their group and advance to the third round, to be played between September and November 2025.<ref>{{cite web|author=Mehluli Sibanda |date=11 June 2025|title=Qualified success! Warrior spirit stuns Cuba|url=https://www.royalgazette.com/football/sport/article/20250611/brave-bermuda-through-to-final-round-of-world-cup-qualifiers/|website=The Royal Gazette|location=|publisher=|access-date=16 October 2025}}</ref> Drawn in Group B with [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]], [[Curaçao national football team|Curaçao]] and [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]], the ''Gombey Warriors'' suffered four defeats in their first four matches and found themselves eliminated from qualifying for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.<ref>{{cite web|author=<!-- not stated -->|date=15 October 2025|title=Jamaica Football Team Defeat Bermuda 4 – 0|url=https://bernews.com/2025/10/oct15jamaica-football-team-defeat-bermuda-4-0/|website=Bernews|location=|publisher=|access-date=15 October 2025}}</ref>

=== Coaching history ===
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
* {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Graham Adams]]
* {{flagicon|FRG}} [[Rudi Gutendorf]] (1968)
* {{flagicon|FRG}} [[Bernd Fischer (football coach)|Bernd Fischer]] (1969–??)
* {{flagicon|BER}} Carton Dill (1978-??)
* {{flagicon|BER}} Roderick "Roddy" Burchall (1987–??)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Gary Darrell]] (19??–1992)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Burkhard Ziese]] (1994–1997)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Clyde Best]] (1997–1999)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Robert Calderon]] (1999–2000)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Mark Trott]] (2001)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Kenny Thompson (Bermudian footballer)|Kenny Thompson]] (2004–??)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Kyle Lightbourne]] (2007)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Keith Tucker (Bermudian footballer)|Keith Tucker]] (2008)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Devarr Boyles]] (2011–2012)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Andrew Bascome]] (2012–2016)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Dennis Brown (Bermudian footballer)|Dennis Brown]] (2013–2016)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Kyle Lightbourne]] (2017)
* {{flagicon|BER}} [[Devarr Boyles]] (2017)
*{{flagicon|BER}} [[Andrew Bascome]]  (2017–2018)
*{{flagicon|BER}} [[Dennis Brown (Bermudian footballer)|Dennis Brown]] (2017–2018)
*{{flagicon|BER}} [[Kyle Lightbourne]] (2018–2023)
*{{flagicon|CAN}} [[Michael Findlay (soccer)|Michael Findlay]] (2023–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] against [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] on 25 March 2026.<ref>{{cite news |title=Our SMNT Squad is locked in for their international friendly match vs DR Congo |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=934534565609889&locale=en_GB |access-date=22 March 2026 |work=Facebook |publisher=Bermuda Football Association |date=20 March 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 26 March 2026, after the match against [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]]''.

{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|15 October 2025}}<ref name="NFT">{{cite web|author=<!-- not stated --> |date=|title=Bermuda (2025)|url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/23/2025/Bermuda.html|website=National Football Teams|location=|publisher=|access-date=17 October 2025}}</ref>
: ''Statistics include official FIFA-recognised matches only.''
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Bermuda.''

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"

{|class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=2|CONCACAF Championship / Gold Cup history
|-
!First match
|{{fb|HAI}} 2–1 {{fb-rt|BER}}<br>(16 June 2019; [[San José, Costa Rica|San José]], Costa Rica)
|-
!Biggest win 
|{{fb-rt|BER}} 2–0 {{fb|NCA}}<br>(24 June 2019; [[Harrison, New Jersey|Harrison]], United States)
|-
!Biggest defeat 
|{{fb|HAI}} 2–1 {{fb-rt|BER}}<br>(16 June 2019; [[San José, Costa Rica|San José]], Costa Rica)<br>{{fb|CRC}} 2–1 {{fb-rt|BER}}<br>(20 June 2019; [[Frisco, Texas|Frisco]], United States)
|-
!Best result
|rowspan=2|Group stage ([[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]])
|-
!Worst result
|}

{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=2|CONCACAF Nations League history
|-
!First match
|{{fb-rt|BER}} 1–4 {{fb|PAN}}<br>(5 September 2019; [[Hamilton, Bermuda|Hamilton]], Bermuda)
|-
!Biggest Win
|{{fb-rt|BER}} 6–1 {{fb|DMA}}<br>(12 October 2024; [[Devonshire Parish]], Bermuda)
|-
!Biggest Defeat
|{{fb|DOM}} 6–1 {{fb-rt|BER}} <br>(19 November 2024; [[Santiago de los Caballeros]], Dominican Republic)
|-
!Best Result
|6th – League B ([[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League#League B|2024–25]])
|-
!Worst Result
|Relegation League B ([[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League#League A|2019–20]])
|}

==Honours==
===Subregional===
* '''[[Football at the Pan American Games|Pan American Games]]'''
** {{silver2}} Silver medal (1): [[Football at the 1967 Pan American Games|1967]]<ref name="Winnipeg"/>

* '''[[Football at the Central American and Caribbean Games|Central American and Caribbean Games]]'''
** {{bronze3}} Bronze medal (2): [[Football at the 1974 Central American and Caribbean Games|1974]]<ref name="CAG74"/>, [[Football at the 1978 Central American and Caribbean Games|1978]]<ref name="CAG78"/>

===Friendly===
* '''[[Football at the Island Games|Island Games]] (1)''': [[Football at the 2013 Island Games – Men's tournament|2013]]<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.natwestislandgames2013results.com/files/Football.pdf |title=Football |publisher=NatWestIslandGames}}</ref>
